content.full_text:
July 10, 1992

Attorney General's Office  
Public Inquiry Unit  
P.O. Box 944-255  
Sacramento, CA 94244-2550

My inquiry is how I may use left over campaign funds:

The situation: I was a candidate for the Community College Board in San Francisco in 1988. I was not elected. I have about $200 left in my campaign fund. The Fair Political Practices office tells me that since the money was contributed before 1989 it does not fall in their jurisdiction, but rather the Attorney General's Office. What may I do with the money? For example,

May I use the money to attend political events?

May I give the money to candidates? Only candidates for the Community College Board?

May I use the money to put on a campaign event for another candidate?

Your response will be eagerly awaited. I am now confident that I will not seek another political office.

Sincerely yours,

Bernice Biggs  
708 Second Avenue  
San Francisco, CA 94118

Telephone: 221-5877
